steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, ${m}^{5}{q}^{9}{z}^{2}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Group factors by base
steps.content: Rewrite the product so like bases sit together: $(m^4 \cdot m^1)(q^3 \cdot q^6)(z^{-1} \cdot z^3)$.

steps.title: Add exponents for same bases
steps.content:
Using $a^m \cdot a^n = a^{m+n}$:
• $m$: $4 + 1 = 5$
• $q$: $3 + 6 = 9$
• $z$: $-1 + 3 = 2$
The product simplifies to $m^5 q^9 z^2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• "${m}^{4}{q}^{18}{z}^{-3}$": Multiplies exponents instead of adding them by base - wrong.
  • "${m}^{5}{q}^{9}{z}^{2}$": Exponents $5$, $9$, $2$ match the sums.
  • "${m}^{6}{q}^{6}{z}^{-1}$": Uses the wrong exponent pattern for each variable instead of adding matching bases - wrong.
  • "${m}^{24}{q}^{12}{z}^{-2}$": Mixes exponent operations instead of adding exponents with the same base - wrong.
That matches the correct answer.
